pagePlan.title: Tack Timetable
pagePlan.contentBrief: Project overview: what the product is, who uses it, the AI-assisted scheduling workflow, key value propositions (local AI agent + sandboxed Python execution + deterministic validation). Include quick links to architecture, getting started, and the main features.

pagePlan.title: Architecture
pagePlan.contentBrief: End-to-end architecture with Mermaid diagrams. Cover the 5 layers: Frontend/UI, AI Local Agent Pipeline (6 stages), Python Execution & Sandbox, Electron Desktop Bridge, and API surface. Show data flow from user input → AgentInputPayload → 6-stage loop → sandboxed execution → validated schedule → UI rendering. Explain the security model (no raw LLM code execution on host).

pagePlan.title: Getting started
pagePlan.contentBrief: Prerequisites, install steps (npm install + Python venv), dev workflow (npm run dev + pre-sync scripts), build, lint, test commands. Electron desktop run. Sandbox setup notes (Docker vs bubblewrap). Provider configuration for LLM usage.

pagePlan.title: Glossary
pagePlan.contentBrief: Project-specific terms: Local Agent, 6-stage pipeline (Translator/Planner/Coder/Sandbox/Validator/Repair), ConstraintSpec, ConstraintKind (the 46 kinds), Solver skeleton, Deterministic validator, Round-trip check, Sandbox (Docker/bubblewrap), AgentInputPayload, TokenBudgetGuard, WorkspaceBoard, Quick import format, Solver status (optimal/feasible/infeasible).

pagePlan.title: By the Numbers
pagePlan.contentBrief: Quantitative snapshot: lines of code by language, file counts (source vs test vs config), major subsystems, recent churn hotspots (especially the 17 new constraints + validator overhaul), test coverage signals, build artifact sizes if relevant. Use Mermaid xychart-beta for language breakdown. Note the large deletion of repomix-output.xml.
relatedPages: lore.md — historical context for the growth and recent constraint expansion

pagePlan.title: Tack Timetable — Project Lore
pagePlan.contentBrief: Narrative history of the codebase. Key eras: initial Next.js + OR-Tools prototype, introduction of the 6-stage prompt-driven Local Agent, addition of sandboxing (Docker then bubblewrap), the major constraint system expansion (17 new kinds in one commit), removal of repomix artifact, hardening of prompt sync and CI. Longest-standing features (core agent loop, solver skeleton pattern). Deprecated experiments if any can be identified from git history.

pagePlan.title: Tack Timetable — Fun Facts
pagePlan.contentBrief: Interesting trivia: the 17k-line repomix-output.xml that was added then deleted in one major cleanup, the 46 constraint kinds (many with very specific Vietnamese-school scheduling semantics), the strict 'impact analysis before every edit' culture enforced via GitNexus, the dual web + Electron distribution with a bundled PyInstaller binary, the fact that the AI never runs its own generated code on the host machine.

pagePlan.title: How to Contribute
pagePlan.contentBrief: High-level guide to contributing: where to start, the mandatory impact analysis rule, PR process, definition of done (tests + prompt validation + lint). Link to the detailed sub-pages.

pagePlan.title: Development Workflow
pagePlan.contentBrief: Branching, coding, testing, PR, merge cycle. Mandatory pre-commit steps (npm run lint, npm test, prompt validation). How prompt changes are treated as first-class behavioral changes. How to run the full agent loop locally (dev server + provider keys).

pagePlan.title: Testing
pagePlan.contentBrief: Test frameworks and patterns: Node built-in test runner (tsx --test) for TypeScript, pytest for Python layer. Prompt model validation script. Provider smoke tests. Dataset API tests. How to add tests for new constraint kinds or agent stages. Coverage expectations.

pagePlan.title: Debugging
pagePlan.contentBrief: Common failure modes in the agent pipeline and how to debug them: translator producing wrong ConstraintKind, Coder generating invalid Python, sandbox execution timeouts, deterministic validator false positives, token budget exhaustion. Logging, event tracing via onEvent, Python stdout/stderr capture, round-trip failure diagnostics.

pagePlan.title: Patterns and conventions
pagePlan.contentBrief: Cross-cutting coding patterns: the strict GitNexus impact analysis rule before any symbol edit, bounded agent loops with typed events, security-first execution (sandbox always), prompt-driven behavior (prompts/*.md as source of truth), pragmatic TypeScript (any allowed only at Python JSON boundary), deterministic validation after every solver run, no feature flags (runtime UI config instead).

pagePlan.title: Tooling
pagePlan.contentBrief: Build system (Next.js standalone + electron-builder), prompt sync scripts (sync_prompts.mjs, presync:skeleton), skeleton sync, provider smoke test harness, concurrency test, GitNexus code intelligence tools (mandatory before edits), linting (very relaxed rule set by design).

pagePlan.title: Systems
pagePlan.contentBrief: Overview of internal architectural systems (AI pipeline, Python execution, validation). Explain that these are the core building blocks that do not map 1:1 to user-facing features.

pagePlan.title: AI Pipeline
pagePlan.contentBrief: The 6-stage Local Agent (Translator → Planner → Coder → Sandbox → Validator → Repair). Orchestration in runLocalAgent, token budgeting, bounded retries, violation-driven repair loop, event emission. This is the central innovation of the project.

pagePlan.title: Translator Stage
pagePlan.contentBrief: Natural language constraints → structured ConstraintSpec array (46 kinds). Prompt in prompts/translator.system.md. Fallback parser rules added with the 17 new constraint kinds. Error handling and confidence scoring.

pagePlan.title: Planner Stage
pagePlan.contentBrief: Produces a Plan: decision variables, domain size estimation, constraint ordering, reification needs, objective choice, template selection, risks. Prompt in prompts/planner.system.md.

pagePlan.title: Coder Stage
pagePlan.contentBrief: Emits executable Python solver code using the solver skeleton template. Skeleton injection, AST/syntax checks before execution. Prompt in prompts/coder.system.md. Bounded retries (MAX_CODER_RETRIES=3).

pagePlan.title: Validation Stage
pagePlan.contentBrief: Post-execution deterministic validation. validateSchedule, CP-SAT round-trip check, per-constraint checkers in validator_engine.py. Hard vs soft violations, iisConstraintIds, conflicting constraints detection. Critical for trust in the output.

pagePlan.title: Repair Stage
pagePlan.contentBrief: Violation-driven repair loop. applyRepairPatches + runRepairTurn. MAX_RUNTIME_REPAIR_ROUNDS=1, MAX_VIOLATION_REPAIR_ROUNDS=2. Re-invokes Coder/Validator with context about previous failures.

pagePlan.title: Python Execution System
pagePlan.contentBrief: The secure execution host. code_executor.py (temp dir, py_compile, timeout, result.json contract). Sandbox options (Docker recommended, bubblewrap fallback). IPC bridge from Electron and HTTP fallback from web. Never execute LLM code on host.

pagePlan.title: Validation System
pagePlan.contentBrief: Python-side validator engine (validator_engine.py) that implements checkers for all 46 ConstraintKind values. Used both inside the agent loop and for standalone verification. Relationship to the TypeScript deterministic-validator.

pagePlan.title: Features
pagePlan.contentBrief: User-visible and developer-visible capabilities that span multiple systems. Brief overview of the two primary features: the Scheduling Wizard (interactive canvas) and the Constraint System (natural language + structured constraints with 46 kinds).

pagePlan.title: Scheduling Wizard
pagePlan.contentBrief: The main interactive UI in TimetableApp.tsx (~3k LOC). Assignment entry, quick import text format, constraint input (required/preferred), live timetable grid, Excel export, provider settings modal. How the UI drives the Local Agent pipeline.

pagePlan.title: Constraint System
pagePlan.contentBrief: The 46 built-in ConstraintKind values, their semantics (teacher, subject, class, assignment, session, resource, conditional), severity (hard/soft/info), and automatic checkers. Natural language parsing via Translator + fallback rules. The major expansion from the cdac5b5 commit. How constraints flow through Planner → Coder → Validator → Repair.

pagePlan.title: Reference
pagePlan.contentBrief: Configuration, data models, and external dependencies. Index page with links to the three sub-pages.

pagePlan.title: Configuration
pagePlan.contentBrief: All runtime configuration surfaces: LLM provider settings (baseURL, apiKey, model per stage), timeout values, sandbox mode (TT_SANDBOX_MODE), environment variables consumed by CI and the app. No feature flags.

pagePlan.title: Data Models
pagePlan.contentBrief: Core TypeScript interfaces that cross the entire system: AgentInputPayload, ConstraintSpec + ConstraintKind (all 46), Plan, ScheduleEntry, Violation, DeterministicValidationReport, ExecutionResult, LocalAgentFinalResult, AIProviderConfig. Python-side equivalents where they exist.

pagePlan.title: Dependencies
pagePlan.contentBrief: Key external dependencies and why they exist: Radix UI + Tailwind + shadcn/ui (frontend), Zustand + React Query (state), OpenAI SDK (LLM), xlsx (export), zod (validation), ortools (Python solver), Docker/bubblewrap (sandbox). Note on the relaxed ESLint rules due to TS ↔ Python boundary.

pagePlan.title: Maintainers
pagePlan.contentBrief: Subsystem ownership derived from git blame on the default branch (master). Likely low contributor count (solo or very small team). Table mapping major directories (AI pipeline, Python layer, UI, sandbox, CI) to recent active contributors. No CODEOWNERS file present.
relevantSourcePaths: git blame / git log --follow on key directories

notesForSubAgents: All pages must use full repository-root paths in backticks for source file references (e.g., `src/features/timetable/ai/local-agent.ts`, not just `local-agent.ts`). Every domain page must start with an 'Active contributors' byline derived from git blame on the default branch (exclude bots). Cross-link aggressively using relative markdown links. Do not duplicate content that belongs on another page listed here.
